etymology.description: 子(子) (zǐ, "son") + 系(系) (xì, "continue") = generation continuing through sons -> descendant
etymology.interpretation: In 孫(孫), 子(子) (zǐ, "son") represents the notion of offspring, while 系(系) (xì, "continue") signifies the continuation of a family line. Together, they combine to depict a descendant or grandson, emphasizing the idea of lineage and continuity through male progeny.
etymology.simplification: 系(系) (xì, "continue") was replaced by 小(小) (xiǎo, "small") to create 孙(孙), keeping the 子(子) (zǐ, "son") component intact.

examples.chinese: 他是我的小孙子(他是我的小孫子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì wǒ de xiǎo sūnzi.

examples.chinese: 这家族的孙辈都住在城里(這家族的孫輩都住在城裡)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiāzú de sūn bèi dōu zhù zài chéng lǐ.
examples.english: The descendants of this family all live in the city.

examples.chinese: 爷爷最喜欢和孙女玩耍(爺爺最喜歡和孫女玩耍)。
examples.pinyin: Yéye zuì xǐhuān hé sūnnǚ wánshuǎ.
examples.english: Grandpa loves to play with his granddaughter the most.
